item.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: CloudFlare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

item.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures.

item.title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

item.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.

item.title: Poland says hackers breached water treatment plants, and the US is facing the same threat
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A report by Poland’s top intelligence agency accused Russia of sabotage and hacking activities against the country’s military and civilian infrastructure.

item.title: US defense contractor who sold hacking tools to Russian broker ordered to pay $10M to former employers
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Former cybersecurity executive Peter Williams stole several surveillance and hacking tools and sold them for $1.3 million to a Russian broker that works with Putin’s government.

item.title: Pentagon releases UFO files on new website
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The initial set of files housed on the site will include those containing unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) videos, photos, and original source documents from across the entire U.S. government. The materials have been reviewed for security purposes, but many have "not yet been analyzed for resolution of any anomalies," the Department's statement read.

item.title: Truecaller slashes 70 jobs amid declining ad sales
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Truecaller's ad revenue declined by 44%, leading to the layoffs.

item.title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Learn what it takes to build a Series A in 2027 from top VCs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place at San Francisco’s Moscone West on October 13-15. Register today to hear this session live on the Builders Stage.

item.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

item.title: Lime, the Uber-backed micromobility company, files for IPO
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: After years of hints and preparation, Lime plans to enter the public markets.

item.title: The biggest US power grid is under strain from AI — and no one is happy
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: PJM Interconnection — which oversees the grid for some of the densest data center developments on Earth — wants to overhaul itself. Not everyone thinks it's up to the task.

item.title: Airbnb says AI now writes 60% of its new code
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Airbnb also said its customer support AI bot now handles 40% of issues without escalating to a human agent.

item.title: The fax machine is the bottleneck in US healthcare, and VCs are starting to notice
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. For now, the founders say the administrative staff they work with aren't worried about that; they're more worried about drowning.

item.title: Ramp in talks to hit $40B+ valuation, 6 months after reaching $32B
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The company is reportedly in talks to raise another $750 million at a pre-money valuation of more than $40 billion. It last raised in November at $32 billion.

item.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The new features could be handy for customer service systems, but OpenAI says they have applications that work across a variety of other fields, including education and creator platforms.

item.title: Kodiak AI raises $100M at a steep discount, sending its stock tumbling 37%
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The company made a series of other announcements during earnings, including a new commercial contract, a pilot program in Canada, and a collaboration.

item.title: Disney looking to make a unified ‘super app,’ report says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro, who took over for Bob Iger earlier this year, has emphasized his intent to streamline the Disney experience.

item.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: AI startup Pit is led by the co-founders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

item.title: Hackers deface school login pages after claiming another Instructure hack
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai, Zack Whittaker
item.description: The cybercrime group ShinyHunters claimed to have hacked Instructure again, defacing the login pages of several Instructure customer schools with an extortion message.
